title: Assertion fail with chparam.
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.33+103 (git sha1 aeb742b8b, gcc 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Run `yosys -p "read_verilog -defer top.v" -p "hierarchy -top top -chparam p 1"` with the file

`top.v`:
```verilog
module top #(parameter p=1) ();
wire x [1:0]; 
empty e(x[0]);
endmodule

module empty(input wire y);
endmodule 
```

### Expected Behavior

Successful elaboration.

### Actual Behavior

Assertion failed:
```
-- Running command `read_verilog -defer top.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: top.v
Parsing Verilog input from `top.v' to AST representation.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\top'.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\empty'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

-- Running command `hierarchy -top top -chparam p 1' --

2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).
Parameter \p = 1

3. Executing AST frontend in derive mode using pre-parsed AST for module `\top'.
Parameter \p = 1
Generating RTLIL representation for module `$paramod\top\p=32'00000000000000000000000000000001'.

3.1. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top

3.2. Executing AST frontend in derive mode using pre-parsed AST for module `\empty'.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\empty'.
Reprocessing module top because instantiated module empty has become available.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `$paramod\top\p=32'00000000000000000000000000000001'.
ERROR: Assert `modules_.count(module->name) == 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:690.
```

The execution proceeds without error if any of the following is changed:

- no `-defer` in `read_verilog`
- no `chparam` in `hierarchy`
- no array indexing in the `top` module

Might be related to #3741
